what_it_does: Implements a file-discovered registry for DECISION definitions and a pure mark-folding engine that resolves decision state and definition updates from immutable mark threads, plus an operator inbox feed and a decide signal emitter.
observations: Discovers decision rows from decisions/<id>.py files declaring a module-level DECISION dict, using importlib.util.spec_from_file_location to avoid importing the decisions directory as a package.
Validates discovered decisions fail-loud via _build_decision; malformed entries raise DecisionError rather than being skipped.
compose_state derives pending/decided state from decision_take and decision_retract marks, with the latest-by-ts winning and the row never mutating.
compose_definition folds accepted decision_update marks onto the row using a whitelist of content fields and sets a reopened flag when options change on an already-decided card.
pending_decision_updates surfaces the latest unactioned proposal per whitelisted field so the operator sees one pending update at a time.

decision_inbox projects discovered decisions into stack-item records, composing definition/state, canonicalizing addresses, and resolving owner/required_elements/server-side element texts through an optional duck-typed subtype registry.
decision_decided_signal emits a decision.decided run record and optionally a cc_board file_item notice attached to the decision address, without live dispatch or wake.
Includes an operational self-test under __main__ covering discovery, validation, malformed-fail-loud, compose_state, and canonical address normalization.
standouts: The compose_definition/compose_state coupling detects Hole-1 stale decisions when accepted updates touch options on a decided card.
Owner is resolved from the subtype registry rather than authored per row, with only a rare row-level override, to prevent drift.
SERVER_SIDE_ELEMENT_TEXTS is imported from runtime.decision_memory inside decision_inbox to wire static subtype element texts into the feed.
The signal function deliberately stays floor-clean by writing records/files only and explicitly not performing channel_act posts or wakes.
The module is named decision_registry.py instead of decisions.py to avoid shadowing the decisions/ namespace package.
conventions: Mirrors the LifterRegistry/MarkTypeRegistry file-discovery pattern: os.listdir, spec_from_file_location by path, validate, register.
Registry-is-truth: the discovered file set is the authoritative decision set; no hand-maintained lists.
Pure folds: state and effective definition are resolved views computed from marks, not stored mutations.
Fail-loud validation at discovery for malformed declared rows.
Duck-typing for store (marks_for) and subtype_registry (get/owner/required_elements) to keep decision_registry decoupled.
Uses a dataclass with property accessors to wrap the raw DECISION spec.
concerns: Timestamps are compared lexicographically as strings via max(... key=lambda m: str(m.get('ts') or '')); mixed ISO formats or missing timestamps can misorder or produce arbitrary winners.
decision_decided_signal catches a bare Exception around the board notice, which is surfaced but broad.
decision_inbox imports SERVER_SIDE_ELEMENT_TEXTS inside a try/except, hiding a runtime dependency and silently dropping element texts if the import or registry access fails.
compose_state returns {'row': row} when row is not a dict, which downstream callers may not expect.
The __main__ self-test imports contracts.address, so running the file directly will fail if that module is unavailable.
notes: This is the company-side resolver target for the decision:// scheme: a file-discovered, fail-loud registry plus pure mark-folds that turn immutable decision files and mark threads into resolved views and operator signals.
questions: Where is the JSON schema at schemas/vi-vision/v1/decision.schema.json enforced, given the code performs its own manual structural validation?
How does runtime/cognition.py's resolve_address dispatch to this registry, and what does it do when .get returns None?
What is the exact shape of the suite argument passed to decision_decided_signal, particularly emit_run_record?
Is runtime.decision_memory.SERVER_SIDE_ELEMENT_TEXTS static or itself file-discovered, and where is it authored?
How are decision_update marks generated by the RHM and what ensures their ts values are accepted by the operator?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a file-discovered decision registry and a pure state composer for the decision surface.
apparent_themes: Append-only audit trail with state derived from marks
Fail-loud validation and never-silent malformed handling
Separation of immutable decision definition from resolved state
Operator-in-the-loop decision surface with cross-session visibility
Avoidance of namespace shadowing and hardcoded lists
intention_signals: The file is trying to build an auditable, operator-owned decision system where decision identity is declared as files on disk, resolution is computed from immutable marks, and the system surfaces pending and resolved decisions to UI and explanation layers without hardcoding card behavior.
novelty: The coupling of definition updates with state reopening to catch stale decided values, the subtype-driven resolution of owner and required card elements in the inbox, and the dual event-plus-board resume signal emitted on a decision take.
contribution: A reusable decision registry and mark-fold engine that discovers decision definitions from files, validates them loudly, resolves pending/decided state and accepted definition updates from mark threads, surfaces an operator inbox, and emits structured resume signals when decisions are taken.
summary_for_embedding: A file-discovered registry and pure mark-folding engine for operator decisions, resolving pending/decided state and definition updates from immutable mark threads and surfacing an inbox plus decide signals.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a registry-true, auditable decision surface where decision identity is declared on disk and resolution state is derived from append-only marks.

symbols.does: Exception class for malformed decision rows.
symbols.description: Subclass of ValueError raised by _build_decision when a discovered DECISION dict violates required shape or schema, enforcing fail-loud discovery.

symbols.does: Dataclass wrapper for a discovered decision spec.
symbols.description: Holds the validated DECISION dict and exposes typed property accessors for meaning, options, scope, explanation_source, and legibility without mutating the underlying spec.

symbols.does: Returns the decision's human-readable question.
symbols.description: Property accessor returning the required meaning string from the decision spec.

symbols.does: Returns the list of decision options.
symbols.description: Property accessor returning the required options list from the decision spec.

symbols.does: Returns the decision scope, defaulting to global.
symbols.description: Property accessor returning scope from the spec or 'global' when absent.

symbols.does: Returns the optional explanation source address.
symbols.description: Property accessor returning the explanation_source field from the spec, or None.

symbols.does: Returns the optional legibility metadata dict.
symbols.description: Property accessor returning the legibility field from the spec, or None.

symbols.does: Loads a decision file as a module by path.
symbols.description: Uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location with a synthetic module name to exec a .py decision file and return its stem and module object, avoiding package import of the decisions directory.

symbols.does: Validates a declared DECISION dict and builds a Decision instance.
symbols.description: Fail-loud structural gate checking required fields, id/name match, known fields, non-empty options with labels, scope validity, and legibility shape; raises DecisionError on any malformed entry.

symbols.does: Folds decision_take/retract marks into a resolved state view.
symbols.description: Pure function that returns the row augmented with state, decided_value, decided_by, decided_at, and recommended_label; the latest mark by ts wins, with decision_retract returning the decision to pending.

symbols.does: Folds accepted decision_update marks onto the decision definition.
symbols.description: Pure function that overlays the latest accepted whitelisted field updates onto the row and returns the effective definition plus a reopened flag when options changed on an already-decided card.

symbols.does: Lists pending RHM-proposed decision updates per field.
symbols.description: Pure function surfacing the latest unaccepted/unrejected decision_update proposal for each whitelisted field, sorted by ts, so the operator sees one pending proposal per field.

symbols.does: Builds the operator inbox feed of all discovered decisions.
symbols.description: Iterates the registry, composes accepted definition updates and state from store marks, resolves canonical addresses, names, recommended labels, owner, required_elements, and server-side element texts via an optional duck-typed subtype registry, returning stack-item-shaped records.

symbols.does: Emits a decision.decided signal after a take mark.
symbols.description: Writes an event record via suite.emit_run_record and optionally posts a cc_board file_item notice attached to the decision address; returns the signal dict, surfacing board errors without breaking the event.

symbols.does: File-discovered registry of DECISION modules.
symbols.description: Maintains a mapping of decision ids to validated Decision objects, loading them from disk via spec_from_file_location and providing dict-like access and record projection.

symbols.does: Initializes an empty decision map.
symbols.description: Constructor creating the internal decisions dictionary.

symbols.does: Discovers and registers decision files from directories.
symbols.description: Scans each directory for non-underscore .py files, loads them as modules, and registers any declaring a DECISION dict; returns self for chaining.

symbols.does: Clears and rediscovers the registry from directories.
symbols.description: Resets the internal map and reruns discover so removed files unregister and new files register.

symbols.does: Validates and stores a decision under its id.
symbols.description: Calls _build_decision and assigns the resulting Decision to self.decisions[name].

symbols.does: Returns the declared decision spec dict for an id.
symbols.description: Looks up the Decision wrapper and returns a copy of its spec dict, or the provided default if absent; used by the resolver to obtain the pending definition.

symbols.does: Returns all discovered decision specs as plain dicts.
symbols.description: Collects copies of each registered Decision spec, sorted by id, for surfaces that need the declared definition set.

symbols.does: Dict-like access returning the Decision wrapper.
symbols.description: Allows registry[did] to retrieve the Decision object directly.

symbols.does: Dict-like membership test for a decision id.
symbols.description: Allows did in registry to test registration.

symbols.does: Iterates over registered decision ids.
symbols.description: Allows iteration over the registry keys.

symbols.does: Returns the count of registered decisions.
symbols.description: Allows len(registry) to report the number of discovered decisions.
